title: LM_LOAD
text: LM_LOAD This option specifies how Uniface tries to check out Sentinel licenses. LM_LOAD= { S | sentinel | snt } | { C } { R } { G } { 9 | 10 } S | sentinel | snt —this is the default and is equivalent to CRG on Windows and Linux and CR on other platforms. Commuter licenses are tried first, then the RMS License Manager is tried. If that fails, on Windows and Linux Grace licenses are tried. C —instructs Uniface to look for a Commuter license. R —instructs Uniface to look for a license from the RMS License Manager(s). G —instructs Uniface to look for a Grace license. Grace licenses are granted automatically, if included in your Entitlement. 9 | 10 —instructs Uniface to use Sentinel version 9 or version 10. Both versions of the Sentinel libraries are included in the product. Version 9 is set by default, unless a Cloud LM license is used, which will set this to 10 . Note: The user can use LM_LOAD=10 on all OS platforms. If LM_LICENSE_FILE specifies an RMS License Manager, you can specify one or more single letters ( C , R , G ) to control which type of license is tried and the order in which Uniface attempts to check out a license. For example, you may wish to disable Commuter and/or Grace licenses if you are sure you will not be needing these license types. This can improve startup performance by eliminating unnecessary checks. To do so, set LM_LOAD=R . This instructs Uniface to only check out a license from the RMS License Manager. Only disable Grace when the application is running on the same machine as the RMS License Manager. This is to ensure that no network issues will cause the RMS License Manager to become unavailable. Note: Grace licenses are supported only on Windows and Linux. If you specify Sentinel or SNT , that must be the only option. When using single letters, you can specify more than one. This disables certain license types, for example CR to disable Grace licenses, or RG to disable Commuter Licenses. If you are specifying the Sentinel version, this must be at the end, for example LM_LOAD=CR10 or LM_LOAD=Sentinel 10 . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
